title: SFD Bryer Code Compliance Software -- No Cost Three-Year Agreement
summary: Deputy Chief Elton Davis presents item 31, a three-year agreement with Bryer for code compliance software used for sprinkler system and fire alarm report submissions. No cost to the city. Council enthusiastically approves.
raw_content:
[00:53:05] item 31 is agreement with uh Bryer. This
[00:53:08] is a code compliant code compliant
[00:53:11] software in which we
[00:53:14] which we use it as a tool to
[00:53:18] for the submission of uh excuse me for
[00:53:22] the submission of uh reports that are
[00:53:25] required for sprinkler systems, fire
[00:53:27] alarms, things on along that line. It's
[00:53:30] a three-year agreement and it's there is
[00:53:33] no cost to the city.
[00:53:35] >> Okay.
[00:53:37] >> Like that.
[00:53:37] >> Like that.
[00:53:38] >> You should have started off with that
[00:53:39] first.
[00:53:40] >> All right.
[00:53:41] >> Absolutely.
[00:53:42] >> Any questions?
[00:53:43] >> Not really.
[00:53:46] >> Thank you.
[00:53:50] >> Afternoon, counselors. First deputy
[00:53:51] chief Rich Trudeell. That is a tough act
[00:53:53] to follow. I'm not asking for money.
processing_notes: Free software for fire code compliance reporting -- sprinkler systems, fire alarms.
Segment 10 of 23 from Common Council Study Session, Wednesday March 18th 2026
Transcript corrections applied: CNS -> CNS Engineers; SU -> Syracuse University
